title: Capability gap assessment by solution component (Build/Buy/Partner)

source.file: input_data/corpus/20200803 PC-Strategy-Solution Development and GoToMarket PlanVer1.5.xlsx

grain: one row per solution component within a solution area
summary: A capability-gap matrix: each row is a solution component (e.g. Omni Channel Intake, Predictive Analytics, Cloud Hosted) within a solution area (Claim / UW / F&A), with a description, a priority rating (Low/Medium/High), a narrative of the current capability/gap, and Build / Buy / Partner Y-N flags. WARNING: the detected header row holds actual data values, so column headers are unreliable (some are very long narrative strings); read contents structurally. Near-duplicate of another sheet in the workbook.
use_cases: Which solution components have capability gaps and in which solution area?
What is the priority rating of a given solution component?
For a component, is the recommended approach Build, Buy, or Partner?
What is the described current capability or gap for a component?

columns.name: Claim
columns.note: solution area: Claim / UW / F&A; header is a data value

columns.name: FNOL-as-a- Service
columns.note: parent solution name; header is a data value; 83% empty

columns.name: Omni Channel Intake
columns.note: solution component name; header is a data value

columns.name: Provides a channel agnostic claim reporting solution for customers (broker and insured)
columns.note: component description; header is a data value

columns.name: Medium
columns.note: priority rating Medium/High/Low; header is a data value

columns.name:
-Brokers and Insureds want the choice of reporting channels (Mobile, Web, Email, Fax, Phone) and expect a carrier to provide them with all options 
-Carriers and customers desire FNOL set up (claim # assignment) and adjuster assignment as quickly as possible in order to ensure timely investigation, evaluation reserving and resolution.  Anything more than 4 hours is often perceived as too long leading to adverse impacts on the claim and the customer experience.   
-An FNOL contains a number of data points which are important to capture quickly and accurately so they can be immediately used to predict claim outcomes, properly reserve, report losses to underwriting, price risks and manage aggregate loss costs. 
-Significant effort and cost is needed to convert unstructured, semi-structured and structured documents, embedded files, notes, ACORD forms, scans, images etc...) to useable formats.
-Customers often complain of insufficient transparency into the claim assignment process.  They do not receive timely notification of the claim number nor the adjuster ID leading to unnecessary phone calls and emails and ultimately poor customer satisfaction.  .  
-Customers and regulators require data privacy and security for all PHI, PII information shared when reporting a loss.
columns.note: gap / pain-point narrative; header is a very long data value

columns.name:
Capability exists.  
1. Full scale multi-channel implementation has not been done with any client to date 
2. Mobile interface and reporting does not exist today
columns.note: metrics/benefits narrative; header is a very long data value; mostly empty

columns.name:
TAT Non Voice - 95% assigned within 2 hours (most stringent)

AHT - not always measured.  HIG Measures and standard is 4.30.  (Typical AHT across commercial lines is 10 - 20min)

Quality - 88% - 95% DPU and DPO (typical is 90 - 95% DPU)

%Reassignments
Complaint Ratio

Voice ASA - 70 - 80 % answered within 20 - 25 sec industry might reflect higher)

Abandonment Rate - 2%-7%
columns.note: metrics/benefits narrative; header is a very long data value; mostly empty

columns.name:
-Reduce claim costs - ALAE and ULAE

-Streamline and simplify the process

-Improve accuracy of assignment

-Provide early warning flags for fraud, litigation etc. reducing indemnity and legal cost

-Improve customer experience

-Simplify pricing - price per FNOL - allows for surge capacity and flexibility transferring burden to supplier
columns.note: metrics/benefits narrative; header is a very long data value; mostly empty

examples.Provides a channel agnostic claim report:
Provides insured/broker with the ability to report claims via phone in a workflow enabled call flow using dynamic scripts, triage and adjuster assignment. 
Claims Inquiry and Provider Help lines may also be part of the module.
examples.Capability exists. \n1. Full scale mult:
Offshore Voice FNOL capability exists with entry into client platforms.  Gap:  
1. Front end (Cloud Based) Dynamically scripted FNOL tool to capture loss information to reduce AHT and improve CX
2. No onshore competitive capability

examples.Provides a channel agnostic claim report: Provides ability to capture and report real time on TAT, Volume by LOB, Volume by Channel, Quality etc..)
